README.md.role: `codex-aider-bridge-app` is a local orchestrator that separates **planning and review** from **code execution**.

Universal Pipeline.md.role: The bridge has two roles, played by the same entity (the supervisor):

bridge_logging/__init__.py.role: Logging helpers for the bridge app

models/__init__.py.role: Typed models used across the bridge app

models/task.py.role: defines Task, AiderContext, SubTask, SelectedFiles; exposes id

supervisor/__init__.py.role: Supervisor package — Tech Supervisor agent for planning and review

supervisor/agent.py.role: defines SupervisorError, SupervisorAgent; exposes generate_plan, generate_subplan, review_task

ui/__init__.py.role: Bridge UI package — Flask web interface for the Codex Aider Bridge App

ui/app.py.role: defines _ChatRuntime, SupervisorProxyThread; exposes index, page_dashboard, page_run, page_git, page_knowledge

ui/bridge_runner.py.role: defines BridgeRun; exposes get_run, add_listener, remove_listener, build_command, start

ui/setup_checker.py.role: exposes check_all, check_python, check_aider, check_ollama, check_codex

ui/state_store.py.role: exposes load_settings, save_settings, load_history, add_history_entry, update_history_entry

utils/checkpoint.py.role: Lightweight checkpoint: persist completed task IDs so a failed run can resume

utils/command_resolution.py.role: exposes split_command, resolve_command_arguments, resolve_executable, iter_local_script_directories, build_missing_executable_message

utils/manual_supervisor.py.role: defines ManualSupervisorError, ManualSupervisorSession; exposes submit_review_request, consume_existing_decision, record_completed_review, try_resume_completed_task, wait_for_decision

utils/onboarding_scanner.py.role: Onboarding scanner — one-time source analysis for existing projects

utils/project_knowledge.py.role:
Project knowledge cache — stores what every file does so any AI can
understand the project architecture by reading one JSON file

utils/project_type_prompt.py.role: Interactive project-type selection prompt shown at first bridge run

utils/report_generator.py.role: Generate a Markdown run report from a session token report

utils/run_diagnostics.py.role: Run Diagnostics — structured failure analysis for AI supervisors

utils/token_tracker.py.role: Token usage tracker for the bridge

ui/static/js/core/api.js.role: Fetch JSON from the bridge API.

memory/memory_client.py.role: Import os, logging, urllib.request, urllib.error, json.

docs.title: Codex Aider Bridge App
docs.summary: # Codex Aider Bridge App is a local orchestrator that separates **planning and review** from **code execution**. It supports both: an external supervisor CLI flow ( , , etc.) a manual supervisor flow where the active agent session reviews each task directly and the bridge waits for decision JSON files instead of calling another AI process For the most accurate low-token workflow, use: a pre-written That profile is designed for: Codex supervises, Aider implements, bridge validates. It ships with a **web UI** you can launch by double-clicking , and a CLI you can drive directly from the terminal.

docs.title: AGENTIC AI ONBOARDING DOCUMENT
docs.summary: # AGENTIC AI ONBOARDING DOCUMENT ## Codex-Aider Bridge — Project Brief Read this document fully before doing anything. After reading, you will know exactly what this project is, how it works, your role, and what to do next — all in one prompt. The **Codex-Aider Bridge** is a local Flask web application that separates **AI planning/review** (expensive cloud AI like you) from **code execution** (cheap local Ollama LLM via Aider).

docs.title: AGENT_CONTEXT
docs.summary: # AGENT_CONTEXT ## Architecture Summary The bridge is a local CLI orchestrator with a strict two-role separation: The loop is strictly sequential and acknowledgement-gated: the supervisor must approve each task before the next one starts. A **web UI** ( ) provides a browser-based front end over the same bridge, enabling setup detection, live task progress, and run history without any terminal interaction. --- ## Module Responsibilities ### Core bridge (CLI) Orchestrates repo scanning, plan acquisition, the sequential task-review loop, logging, git-readiness pre-flight, per-task auto-commit, and CLI argument handling.

docs.title: AI Supervisor Reference
docs.summary: # AI Supervisor Reference This document explains how an agentic AI should behave when supervising Aider through this bridge. The recommended operating mode is no longer “spawn another AI CLI and hope it plans well”. The recommended mode is: the active AI session creates the task plan the bridge runs Aider the bridge validates and records results the active AI session reviews each task the bridge resumes from the AI’s decision the bridge keeps a structured project memory under --- ## The Supervisor Role The supervisor is the **technical lead**.

docs.title: Case Study: Codex-Aider Bridge — Separating AI Thinking from AI Coding
docs.summary: # Case Study: Codex-Aider Bridge — Separating AI Thinking from AI Coding **Date:** April 2026 **Duration:** 2 months of active development **Team:** 1 human developer + Claude (Agentic AI supervisor) **Codebase:** ~16,000 lines Python + ~14,000 lines JS/CSS/HTML --- ## 1. The Problem Cloud AI models (Claude, GPT-4) are excellent at planning and reasoning but expensive at scale. A single day of active coding with Claude Opus costs $15-50 in API tokens.

docs.title: Firebase Cloud Sync — Architecture Design
docs.summary: # Firebase Cloud Sync — Architecture Design ## Overview Optional cloud sync that lets users access their bridge data from anywhere via a web dashboard. Each user's data is stored in their own Firestore partition — no data is shared between users. The super admin (you) can see aggregate metrics (user count, project count, token usage) but never project content.

docs.title: Firebase Cloud Sync — Complete Implementation Plan
docs.summary: # Firebase Cloud Sync — Complete Implementation Plan Version: 1.0 | Date: 2026-04-04 Status: DESIGN COMPLETE — Implementation pending --- ## Table of Contents --- ## 1.
